amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The Type III Secretion System (T3SS) in pathogenic E. coli is a molecular syringe that directly injects effector proteins from the bacterial cytoplasm into host cells. This sophisticated nanomachine consists of a basal body spanning both bacterial membranes, an external needle complex, and a translocation pore that inserts into the host membrane. The system is tightly regulated, activating upon host cell contact, and delivers dozens of effector proteins that manipulate host cell signaling, cytoskeleton, and immune responses. This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from T3SS assembly and regulation through effector secretion, translocation into host cells, and the resulting cellular manipulations that facilitate bacterial colonization and pathogenesis.

    I --> L{Basal Body Assembly?}
    J --> L
    K --> L

    R --> S[EscE Needle Tip]

    U --> W{Host Cell Contact?}
    V --> W
    W -->|Yes| X[Contact Sensing]
    W -->|No| Y[Standby Mode]
    
    X --> Z[EspD EspB Translocon]
    Z --> AA[Host Membrane Insertion]
    AA --> AB[Translocation Pore Formation]
    
    AB --> AC{Secretion Hierarchy?}

    AD --> AG[EspB EspD EspA]

    AE --> AH{Effector Selection?}

    AK --> AT{Immune Evasion?}

    AT -->|NleB| AV[Glycosylate Death Receptors]

    AO --> AX[Actin Pedestal]

    AQ --> BA[Membrane Ruffling]
    AR --> BB[Barrier Dysfunction]

    AU --> BD[Reduced Cytokine Secretion]
    AV --> BE[/Apoptosis Inhibition/]
    AW --> BF[Inflammation Suppression]
    
    AZ --> BG{Pathogenic Outcome?}
    BB --> BG
    BC --> BG
    BD --> BG
    BE --> BG
    BF --> BG
    
    BG -->|Combined Effects| BH[Diarrheal Disease]
    BH --> BI[Attaching & Effacing Lesions]
    
    C --> BJ{Regulatory Network?}
    BJ -->|Ler| C
    BJ -->|GrlA GrlR| BK(\1)
    BJ -->|Quorum Sensing| BL[Density-Dependent]
    
    BK --> C
    BL --> C

name: E. coli Type III Secretion System
notes: Created based on research on EPEC/EHEC Type III Secretion Systems, including structural and functional studies of the LEE pathogenicity island.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on peer-reviewed literature on enteropathogenic and enterohemorrhagic E. coli (EPEC/EHEC) T3SS, including structural studies of the injectisome, regulatory mechanisms, and effector functions. The pathway components, assembly sequence, and logic gates have been validated against primary research sources. The model represents the LEE (Locus of Enterocyte Effacement) encoded T3SS, the best-characterized system in E. coli.

sources.title: Type III secretion systems: bacterial nanomachines for protein delivery into target cells
The LEE-encoded type III secretion system of enteropathogenic Escherichia coli: a model for understanding EPEC pathogenesis
Structure and assembly of the bacterial type III secretion machine
Enteropathogenic E. coli (EPEC) transfers its receptor for intimate adherence into mammalian cells

verifiedBy: Manual creation with PubMed literature review
